repository.branch_rule: Resolve the currently permitted branch namespace from repository rules before branch creation; never push directly to the default branch.
repository.delivery_model: one master plan executed through multiple bounded pull requests

title: EAFIX Single-Source-of-Truth Registry System Master Implementation Plan

objective.primary: Deliver one governed registry system in which every authoritative fact has exactly one declared owner, stable identity, provenance, validation, deterministic projections, and an enforceable change path.
secondary: Harden the existing ten-registry candidate implementation so it is deterministic, cross-reference safe, Python 3.9 compatible, and fully covered by no-write check mode.
Ratify the 34-module identity universe and permanent locators without allowing narrative plans or hand-authored trees to become authority.
Populate and reconcile module, process, contract, artifact, integration, operator, configuration, operational-control, reuse, and decision registries.
Create an explicit field-level authority map so schema files and specialized policy references remain canonical only for the facts they uniquely own.
Cut over routing and document authority only after candidate integrity and authority parity are proven.
Use the registry system to generate and validate the module-centric repository migration, including target tree, path maps, impact maps, rollback maps, and CI enforcement.
objective.completion_definition: The project has one active implementation plan, one machine-readable authority manifest, one canonical owner for every governed fact, complete and valid domain registries, deterministic generated views, a ratified 34-module set, a fully mapped repository, successful staged migration, clean-clone reproducibility, and blocking CI that prevents authority, registry, and structure drift.

core_design_decision.decision: Do not collapse all project data into one monolithic registry file.
core_design_decision.model: Keep bounded domain registries, unify them through a canonical registry authority manifest, cross-registry graph, stable identifiers, and deterministic generated projections.
reason: The existing implementation already separates ten domain registries.
A single giant data file would increase merge conflicts, validation blast radius, and ownership ambiguity.
Single source of truth means one authoritative owner per fact, not one physical file for every fact.
Field-level contract shapes remain canonical in schema files; registries store identities, links, producers, consumers, status, and provenance.

source_plan_consolidation.consolidation_rule: This master plan becomes the sole sequencing and implementation authority after approval. Source plans become provenance, superseded implementation plans, or non-authoritative reference inputs.

sources.role: module identity, naming, packaging, ownership, repository migration, and structural validation track

sources.filename: EAFIX Module Folder Naming and Migration Plan.txt

sources.role: byte-identical duplicate of the naming plan
sources.duplicate_of: EAFIX Module Folder Naming and Migration Plan.txt

sources.role: proposed target visualization and seed expectations
registry engine determinism, candidate integrity, foreign-key baseline, authority-parity shadow, and CI remediation
amendment provenance explaining the v1.0.0 to v1.1.0 changes

routing_and_authority.preflight_reference_warning: The naming-governance file path cited by the source plans was not resolvable at the expected current-master locations during consolidation. PHASE-00 must locate the active file or record it as missing before any naming decision.
authority_priority_before_cutover: Current canonical module and process catalogs
Current specialized platform, UI, channel, calendar, and schema authorities
authority_priority_after_cutover: Registry authority manifest for field-level source selection
Canonical authored domain registry or explicitly registered external authority selected by that manifest
Generated current views and compatibility projections
routing_and_authority.conflict_rule: No candidate registry value, proposed tree, narrative plan, or generated report may override a current authority. A conflict is resolved only by a governed decision with provenance, followed by an explicit authority update or cutover.

problem_register.title: Multiple implementation plans compete for sequencing authority
Two naming plans are byte-identical duplicates
Registry remediation v1.0.0 and its amendment are already superseded by v1.1.0
Candidate registries are internally useful but are not yet the declared authority
Known deterministic-build and validation defects must be repaired before any authority cutover
Authority parity is advisory and unresolved differences are not yet governed
The current 27-module authority and the proposed 34-module universe are not reconciled
Creating standalone ratification and policy JSON files could create new parallel authorities
Module locator, F4 symbol, manifest separator, packaging, schema placement, shared-file, and state decisions remain open
Current file mapping contains multi-module and unmapped ownership records
The proposed 34-module tree is hand-authored and can drift
The source plans do not provide a complete governed authority-cutover procedure
No unified ongoing change policy guarantees that registries, generated views, routing, and repository structure remain synchronized
Some routed reference paths may be stale or missing and must not be silently substituted

registry_authority_manifest.purpose: Declare, at field-group granularity, the one canonical source for each governed fact, the current pointer, schema, owner, cutover state, generated projections, and conflict policy.
registry_authority_manifest.must_not_contain: Duplicated copies of all domain records.

authority_model.external_authority_rule: A specialized external artifact may remain canonical for facts that do not belong inside a registry, such as field-level schema definitions, MT4 platform facts, deterministic calendar rules, or detailed UI behavior. It must be registered in the artifact registry and selected explicitly in the authority manifest. No overlapping registry field may claim the same fact.

canonical_fact_examples.fact_group: module identity, locator, lifecycle, aliases, and relationships
process step identity, order, dependencies, and module bindings
contract identity, versions, producers, consumers, schema links, and status
contract field names, types, constraints, and compatibility
repository path identity, ownership, source/generated/runtime classification, and target disposition
approved architecture, naming, packaging, cutover, and migration decisions
UI entity identities and cross-registry links
detailed UI behavior until migrated by explicit decision

authority_model.no_parallel_authority_rule: Standalone ratification, crosswalk, policy, inventory, and migration files are either immutable approval evidence or generated projections. They must never be independently editable current authorities after cutover.

rules: A record cannot become active without resolvable provenance and required decision references.
Superseded and retired records remain addressable.
Cross-registry references use stable IDs, not paths or display names.
Generated views do not introduce facts absent from authored registries or registered external authorities.

global_constraints.no_fabrication: No module, process, file owner, contract shape, path, port, protocol, policy, or target disposition may be invented. Unknown facts remain needs_review and block affected cutover or migration scope.
global_constraints.one_active_plan: After approval, this JSON is the only active implementation-sequencing plan for the registry system and module migration.
global_constraints.one_authority_per_fact: Every governed fact group has exactly one canonical owner selected by the authority manifest.
global_constraints.determinism: Same approved inputs produce byte-identical outputs on supported platforms.
global_constraints.no_write_check_mode: Check mode performs zero writes and compares every managed generated artifact.
global_constraints.report_only_before_blocking: New parity and structure checks begin in report-only mode and become blocking only after a clean, approved baseline.
global_constraints.write_allowlists: Every task references an explicit write scope. Any path outside that scope is forbidden.
global_constraints.archive_immunity: Archived and superseded areas are excluded from authority and are modified only by an explicit archival task.
global_constraints.branch_safety: Use an allowed non-default branch; no direct push to master.
global_constraints.rollback: Every authority cutover, generated path change, move, split, and compatibility shim has a tested rollback.
global_constraints.schema_single_home: A cross-module contract shape has one canonical schema. Module-local schema folders contain references, projections, examples, or module-private schemas only.
global_constraints.runtime_compatibility: Migration may not change MT4, Python, UI, calendar, transport, broker, packaging, or deployment behavior unless a separately approved functional change is required.
global_constraints.generated_not_hand_edited: Current views, graphs, reports, catalogs, target trees, rename maps, and validation reports are generated and protected from manual edits.
global_constraints.dynamic_baseline: Source-plan counts such as 291 records, 10 registries, PR 74, or a specific merge SHA are provenance hints, not permanent acceptance constants. PHASE-00 re-verifies the current baseline.

allowlist: approved registry JSONL records named in the correction decision
tests and fixtures required for the corrected references
decision/configuration/module/artifact registry records required by the approved migration policy
only source and target paths enumerated in the approved wave map
path consumers enumerated in the approved impact report
forbidden: authority changes not part of the wave

human_decision_register.title: Approve this master plan as the sole implementation sequencing authority
Approve canonical authored registry form
human_decision_register.recommended: JSONL files are authored canonical registries; *.current.json files are generated views

human_decision_register.title: Approve field-level authority manifest model
human_decision_register.recommended: registries own identities and relationships; registered specialized artifacts own field-level facts not suitable for registries

human_decision_register.title: Ratify the 34-module identity universe and permanent M0001-M0034 locators
Resolve manifest filename separator
human_decision_register.recommended: use the currently governed convention once the active naming-governance authority is located

human_decision_register.title: Choose repository Python packaging model
human_decision_register.recommended: workspace with selected independently packaged modules only where runtime evidence requires it

human_decision_register.title: Approve contract schema placement and module-local schema policy
human_decision_register.recommended: canonical cross-module and boundary shapes remain in registered global schema homes; module-local folders contain references, examples, projections, and private schemas

human_decision_register.title: Approve multi-owner and unmapped file dispositions
Approve committed versus runtime-only global .state content

execution_profiles.behavior: Enable blocking module-to-process foreign-key validation in the registry-engine remediation track.
Create a separate governed data-correction track; continue unrelated engine work; do not enable blocking FK enforcement or claim full completion until the baseline is corrected.

pr_delivery_sequence.scope: registry engine determinism and candidate-integrity remediation
SSOT authority manifest, common schemas, and change policy
governed data corrections and conflict decisions
complete domain registry population and cross-registry validation
module/global-root policy records and generated projections
migration generators, validators, and dry-run artifacts
final enforcement, completion record, and operating governance

phases.name: Consolidate plan authority and freeze the verified baseline
phases.purpose: Establish one active plan, verify routing and repository rules, classify every source plan, and capture a read-only baseline before implementation.
entry_conditions: All seven source files are available and hashable.

tasks.title: Verify source-plan hashes and relationships
actions: Hash all source plans.
Confirm the two naming-plan files are byte-identical.
Confirm registry remediation v1.1.0 supersedes v1.0.0 and already incorporates the amendment.
Record source provenance in the master-plan consolidation record.
acceptance: All seven source files have SHA-256 values.
Duplicate and supersession relationships match the source content.
No source plan remains ambiguously active.

tasks.title: Resolve mandatory routing references
actions: Read the routing JSON.
Resolve every required reference path against current master.
Record missing, stale, moved, or ambiguous references.
Locate the active module naming-governance authority or open a blocking governance gap.
acceptance: Every required document has resolved, missing, or needs-review status.
No missing reference is silently replaced.
The naming-governance path is resolved before PHASE-05.

tasks.title: Resolve branch, ruleset, and CI execution constraints
actions: Identify the allowed branch namespace from current repository rules.
Confirm default branch.
Check whether GitHub Actions can start jobs.
Record required checks and environmental blockers.
acceptance: Branch naming is evidence-backed.
Startup-blocked CI is not counted as passing.
No implementation branch is created before this task passes.

tasks.title: Capture registry and authority baseline
actions: Record current authored registry files, schemas, record counts, generated outputs, tool versions, and current pointers.
Run candidate validation and two normal builds without committing changes.
Run an independent read-only module-to-process foreign-key probe.
Record current canonical authorities and their hashes.
acceptance: Baseline counts are measured rather than copied from a source plan.
Exactly one FK execution profile is selected.
The working tree returns to clean state.

tasks.title: Approve this master plan as the sole sequencing authority
actions: Review this plan against the source consolidation record.
Record HD-SSOT-001.
Mark source plans superseded, duplicate, applied amendment, or reference-only as specified.
acceptance: One active implementation plan remains.
Source-plan content is traceable through SHA-256 and preserved disposition.

pass_condition: routing references resolved or explicitly blocked
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop. No registry code, authority, or repository migration changes are allowed.

phases.name: Harden the registry engine and candidate-integrity pipeline
phases.purpose: Absorb the complete registry-remediation v1.1.0 track and make candidate generation deterministic, complete, cross-reference safe, and enforceable.

tasks.title: Implement one pure render pipeline for all managed outputs
actions: Render snapshots, graph, reports, Mermaid, Markdown, and build manifest in memory.
Use one expected-output map for write and check modes.
Build the manifest last.
acceptance: Every managed output is emitted by the same render path.
No generated artifact has separate write/check semantics.

tasks.title: Repair build-manifest integrity and path normalization
actions: Exclude registry_build_manifest.json from its own output hashes.
Store repository-relative POSIX path keys.
Verify every input and output hash.
Reject absolute paths and backslashes.
acceptance: Manifest self-hash absent.
All listed hashes verify.
No platform-dependent path keys.

tasks.title: Implement Python 3.9-compatible stable writes
actions: Use binary writes or Path.open with UTF-8 and LF.
Remove unsupported newline arguments from Path.write_text.
Ensure exact bytes are shared across Python 3.9 and 3.11.
acceptance: Python 3.9 and 3.11 produce identical output bytes.

tasks.title: Complete no-write check mode
actions: Compare every managed generated artifact.
Report missing and different files deterministically.
Perform zero writes, including reports and manifests.
acceptance: Tampering each output class causes failure without rewriting.
A clean baseline returns zero differences.

tasks.title: Implement complete cross-registry validation
actions: Validate all schema-declared ID references.
Apply the selected FK baseline profile.
Separate structural errors from advisory governance findings.
Preserve review and orphan records unless separately resolved.
acceptance: Enabled foreign references resolve.
Dirty baseline follows the data-correction track.
Advisory findings cannot hide structural failure.

tasks.title: Create candidate-integrity regression and mutation suite
actions: Add tests for self-hash, POSIX paths, hash verification, two-build stability, every output class, no-write behavior, FK validation, and current dataset completeness.
Use temporary roots and order-independent tests.
acceptance: Tests pass on Windows/Linux-compatible paths.
Tests leave the repository clean.

tasks.title: Make Registry Candidate Integrity blocking
actions: Create a Python 3.9/3.11 CI matrix.
Remove continue-on-error from structural validation and generated-output parity.
Preserve verified branch filters.
Keep governance review findings advisory.
acceptance: Candidate integrity executes and passes.
Blocked-before-start jobs are not accepted as evidence.

pass_condition: enabled foreign-reference errors = 0 under selected profile
two consecutive builds produce no diff
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop authority work. Preserve logs and fix the registry engine only.

phases.name: Implement genuine authority-parity shadow validation
phases.purpose: Compare candidate registry facts to routed current authorities without changing authority or silently approving mismatches.

tasks.title: Create authority-parity rule schema and mappings
actions: Map candidate fields to routed authority document IDs, paths, keys, and normalization rules.
Require field-level provenance.
Classify unsupported fields not_comparable rather than guessing.
acceptance: Every rule resolves through the routing JSON.
No lower-authority source is hard-coded when a routed authority exists.

tasks.title: Implement pure authority-parity comparator
actions: Load candidate and authority sources read-only.
Support exact_match, normalized_match, compatible_extension, candidate_missing, authority_missing, conflicting_value, not_comparable, and approved_exception.
Never mutate either side.
acceptance: All classifications are deterministic.
conflicting_value can never normalize to a match.

tasks.title: Implement parity CLI and deterministic report
actions: Validate source availability.
Produce a schema-valid report with source inventory, counts, comparisons, severities, and execution errors.
Write only the designated generated report.
acceptance: Comparator execution errors = 0.
Report schema errors = 0.
Two runs are byte-identical.

tasks.title: Add parity tests and advisory CI job
actions: Test normalization, missing sources, conflicts, approved exceptions, no mutation, and deterministic output.
Create separate Registry Authority Parity (Shadow) job.
Require comparator execution while keeping mismatch counts advisory until PHASE-04 decisions.
acceptance: Parity tool executes in CI.
Mismatches remain visible and cannot change authority.

pass_condition: all comparisons cite routed authority or explicit unavailable/not_comparable status
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop. Do not use parity output for decisions or cutover.

phases.name: Define the SSOT authority contract and registry governance
phases.purpose: Create the missing authority layer that turns valid candidate registries into a governed system without creating parallel standalone authorities.

tasks.title: Approve authored-versus-generated registry model
actions: Record HD-SSOT-002.
Declare JSONL registries authored canonical candidates and *.current.json generated projections.
Define current-pointer and compatibility-projection behavior.
outputs: decision registry record for authored/generated model
acceptance: Exactly one authored source exists for each registry.
Generated views are non-editable.

tasks.title: Create registry authority manifest schema
actions: Define field groups, canonical source, source type, owner, schema, current pointer, cutover state, external authority refs, projections, and conflict policy.
Require no overlapping active ownership.
acceptance: Schema rejects duplicate active owners for the same fact group.
Schema supports registered external authorities without duplicating their content.

tasks.title: Create candidate registry authority manifest
actions: Map all governed fact groups to candidate registries or registered specialized authorities.
Mark every mapping pre_cutover.
Register contract schemas, MT4 reference, UI catalog, calendar DES rules, and other retained specialized authorities in artifact registry.
acceptance: Every governed fact group has exactly one proposed owner.
Every external authority is registered and hash-addressable.
No cutover state is active yet.

tasks.title: Publish common registry record and lifecycle policy
actions: Define required common fields, statuses, stable ID reuse prohibition, supersession, provenance, valid-time, decision references, and content hashing.
Define change classes and approval levels.
acceptance: All ten registry schemas compose with the common contract.
Lifecycle transitions are validated.

tasks.title: Define standalone-artifact classification
actions: Classify ratification files, crosswalks, inventories, policies, trees, and migration reports as canonical registry records, immutable approval evidence, or generated projections.
Prohibit independently editable duplicate authority files.
acceptance: Every planned JSON artifact has one classification.
No planned artifact can silently become a second SSOT.

tasks.title: Approve field-level authority model
actions: Record HD-SSOT-003.
Review coverage matrix and specialized-authority boundaries.
Approve cutover criteria and rollback semantics.
acceptance: Authority model is human-approved.
No unresolved overlapping field ownership remains.

exit_gate.on_fail: Stop registry population and authority cutover.

phases.name: Resolve authority-parity conflicts and registry data defects
phases.purpose: Convert advisory mismatch inventory into governed decisions and clean candidate data, while preventing automatic or invented corrections.

tasks.title: Classify every parity mismatch and open review item
actions: Group mismatches by fact group, severity, source authority, and required owner.
Include existing review and orphan items.
Separate tool defects, stale paths, aliases, missing facts, conflicting values, and non-comparable fields.
acceptance: Every mismatch has an owner and required disposition.
No mismatch disappears between reports without a decision.

tasks.title: Resolve dirty module-to-process references
actions: For PROFILE-DIRTY-FK-BASELINE, trace each invalid pair to module and process authorities.
Create correction decisions and update only approved registry records.
Enable blocking FK validation only after probe returns zero.
acceptance: Zero invalid references without deleted or invented values.
FK enforcement becomes blocking.

tasks.title: Resolve aliases, stale paths, and conflicting values
actions: Use routed authority priority.
Record aliases rather than overwriting stable identities.
Correct stale paths using artifact evidence.
Require a decision record for every conflicting value.
acceptance: Every changed value cites authority and decision provenance.
No lower-authority source wins silently.

tasks.title: Define cutover-blocking versus permitted residual classifications
actions: Mark identity, ownership, dependency, contract-link, process, and authority-pointer conflicts blocking.
Allow not_comparable only where the authority manifest deliberately retains an external canonical source.
Require zero candidate_missing for cutover-required fields.
acceptance: Blocking mismatch count is deterministic.
Allowed residuals have explicit field-group ownership and rationale.

tasks.title: Regenerate and review clean parity baseline
actions: Run full candidate integrity and parity comparison.
Review all diffs.
Freeze approved cutover baseline hashes.
acceptance: Blocking mismatch count = 0.
All residual advisory items comply with the approved policy.
Candidate integrity remains clean.

exit_gate.on_fail: No authority cutover. Continue focused data-correction PRs.

phases.name: Ratify the 34-module universe and close naming decisions
phases.purpose: Resolve the 27/28/34 conflict, permanent locators, naming rules, packaging, schema placement, shared ownership policy, and state policy as registry-backed decisions.

tasks.title: Build evidence-backed 27/28/34 module crosswalk
actions: Compare current module catalog, module manifests, vNext catalog, module maps, reconciliation worksheets, file mapping, and existing candidate module registry.
Map legacy IDs and symbols to proposed canonical records.
Do not create or remove modules without an MCR.
acceptance: Every legacy module is mapped, retired with decision, or explicitly blocking.
No duplicate module ID or symbol.

tasks.title: Resolve F4 and active naming-governance authority
actions: Locate the active naming-governance record.
Complete or create the required rename-symbol MCR for F4_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR.
Resolve any collision before ratification.
acceptance: F4 canonical symbol has accepted governance.
No symbol collision remains.

tasks.title: Ratify module IDs, canonical symbols, and permanent locators
actions: Record HD-SSOT-004.
Approve exactly 34 module records.
Store module_locator in each module registry record.
Never derive or reuse locators.
acceptance: active modules = 34
unique module IDs = 34
unique symbols = 34
unique locators = 34

tasks.title: Resolve manifest filename convention
actions: Record HD-SSOT-006.
Reconcile source-plan single-underscore examples with active governance.
Update generator and validator expectations only after decision.
acceptance: One convention is active.
All 34 expected manifest names are derivable deterministically.

tasks.title: Resolve packaging model
actions: Inventory current pyproject, package, workspace, deployment, and test behavior.
Record HD-SSOT-007.
Assign package policy per module profile only when evidence requires it.
acceptance: No duplicate or conflicting package roots.
Imports and build tools have a migration strategy.

tasks.title: Resolve schema placement and global .state policy
actions: Record HD-SSOT-008 and HD-SSOT-010.
Declare canonical cross-module schema homes.
Define module-private schema rules.
Classify committed generated state versus runtime-only state.
acceptance: No canonical schema duplication.
Runtime-only state is excluded from versioned authority.

tasks.title: Resolve multi-owner disposition vocabulary
actions: Approve allowed dispositions: move, split, retain global, compatibility layer, wrapper, archive, runtime exclude.
Define required evidence and rollback for each.
acceptance: Every disposition has validation and rollback requirements.

exit_gate.on_fail: Stop domain completion and repository design.

phases.name: Complete and reconcile all domain registries
phases.purpose: Populate the registry system so identities, links, provenance, ownership, and lifecycle are complete enough to support authority cutover and repository generation.

tasks.title: Complete module registry
actions: Populate all 34 module identities, locators, aliases, statuses, responsibilities, dependencies, process links, service/runtime links, profile refs, and authority refs.
Represent non-process modules without fabricated process steps.
acceptance: 34 active module records.
All module references valid.
No invented step links.

tasks.title: Complete process registry
actions: Populate canonical steps, codes, order, phase, dependencies, module owners, input/output contract IDs, validation controls, and failure controls.
Reconcile process-step and aligned-process authorities.
acceptance: Step IDs and codes unique.
Phase and dependency graph acyclic where required.
Module and contract references valid.

tasks.title: Complete contract registry without duplicating shapes
actions: Register input and output contracts, versions, producers, consumers, process links, schema refs, implementation status, examples, and compatibility policy.
Keep field definitions in canonical schemas.
Record unresolved contracts as blocking for affected buildability.
acceptance: Every contract link resolves.
One canonical schema per shape.
No field-shape duplication.

tasks.title: Complete artifact registry baseline
actions: Inventory every tracked path plus approved runtime/generated exclusions.
Store repository-relative POSIX paths, path type, owner, consumers, source/generated classification, hashes, and authority refs.
Replace absolute Windows paths in mapping inputs.
acceptance: Every tracked path represented once.
No absolute path remains.
Multi-owner/unmapped records are explicit.

tasks.title: Complete integration and operator registries
actions: Register channels, protocols, ports, directions, producers, consumers, contracts, UI entities, APIs, WebSockets, safety controls, and specialized authority refs.
Preserve MT4 and UI authority boundaries.
acceptance: Channel and UI IDs unique.
Ports/protocols match routed authorities or have decisions.
All links resolve.

tasks.title: Complete configuration, operational-control, reuse, and decision registries
actions: Register configuration identities and owners, safety/failure controls, existing reusable capabilities, and all approval decisions from prior phases.
Search capability registry before custom implementation records are accepted.
acceptance: All prior human decisions have stable IDs.
Every custom-solution record explains why reuse was insufficient.
Safety controls have owners and validation refs.

tasks.title: Run full cross-registry completeness and orphan analysis
actions: Generate graph and reports.
Resolve duplicate IDs, broken references, orphaned required records, missing provenance, and incompatible status transitions.
Preserve approved external-authority links.
acceptance: Schema errors = 0.
Duplicate IDs = 0.
Enabled foreign-reference errors = 0.
Unapproved orphans = 0.
Required provenance gaps = 0.

phases.name: Perform governed registry authority cutover
phases.purpose: Make the registry authority manifest and approved domain sources the routed current authority while preserving reversible compatibility projections.

tasks.title: Generate cutover diff and rollback package
actions: Generate exact routing, doc-authority, authority-manifest, current-pointer, and compatibility-projection changes.
Create inverse patch restoring previous authority pointers.
Prove rollback in a temporary branch or worktree.
acceptance: Cutover and rollback patches are deterministic.
Rollback restores the prior authority model without data loss.

tasks.title: Review field-level authority coverage
actions: Confirm every governed fact group has one canonical owner after cutover.
Confirm retained external authorities are registered and non-overlapping.
Confirm old plans and catalogs are classified as generated, supporting, superseded, or compatibility projections.
acceptance: No dual active authority.
No fact group lacks an owner.

tasks.title: Approve authority cutover
actions: Record HD-SSOT-011.
Review candidate integrity, parity baseline, registry completeness, and rollback evidence.
Approve exact commit scope.
acceptance: Human approval references frozen hashes and patch.
No physical module move is included.

tasks.title: Update routing and document authority atomically
actions: Activate registry authority manifest.
Point module/process/artifact/contract/etc. routes to canonical registry sources or registered external authorities.
Reclassify former independent catalogs according to approved compatibility policy.
Register generated current views.
acceptance: All authority pointers resolve.
No stale active path remains.
Candidate registries are no longer described as shadow.

tasks.title: Validate cutover from a clean clone
actions: Clone the cutover commit.
Run routing resolution, candidate integrity, parity, authority coverage, and generated-output checks.
Run rollback rehearsal once more.
acceptance: All checks pass.
Rollback remains executable.
No working-tree drift.

pass_condition: routing and doc authority point to approved sources
exit_gate.on_fail: Apply authority rollback immediately and keep prior authorities active.

phases.name: Encode module-folder, profile, global-root, and root-artifact policy in the registry system
phases.purpose: Convert naming-plan rules and gap-closure policy into registry-backed decisions and generated projections before any physical migration.

tasks.title: Create module folder naming policy records
actions: Encode module root, first-level container, hidden state, nested folder, runtime-constrained folder, locator reuse, and .module-id rules.
Reference the accepted naming decision and active module records.
outputs: decision/configuration registry records for naming policy
acceptance: Names derive deterministically from locator and canonical symbol.
Nested runtime folders remain conventional.

tasks.title: Create module container inventory and profile assignments
actions: Define evidence-backed containers per module.
Assign exactly one primary profile: Python, MT4, mixed Python/MT4, UI full stack, shared kernel, or documentation-only.
Allow approved extensions only.
acceptance: All 34 modules have a profile and container inventory.
Unsupported empty folders are not generated.

tasks.title: Create module-root artifact policy
actions: Define required and optional .module-id, manifest, AGENTS.md, README.md, package metadata, and runtime-specific files.
Tie manifest naming to the approved convention.
acceptance: Every module has deterministic required artifacts.
Prohibited duplicate authority artifacts are identified.

tasks.title: Create global-root policy
actions: Classify architecture, contracts, governance, integration-tests, tools, EAFIX_auth_docs, .github, and .state.
Assign owners, allowed content, generated/runtime status, and migration rules.
acceptance: Every retained global root has one owner and purpose.
Global roots cannot become dumping grounds.

tasks.title: Generate policy projections and validate
actions: Generate human-readable naming and layout docs from registry data.
Validate 34 expected root names and manifest names.
Compare only as a diagnostic against the proposed hand-authored tree.
acceptance: Generated projections contain no independent facts.
All policy checks pass.

exit_gate.on_fail: No path mapping or physical migration.

phases.name: Map every repository path and resolve ownership
phases.purpose: Make artifact registry coverage complete and approve one evidence-backed disposition for every tracked path.

tasks.title: Scan tracked repository paths and consumers
actions: Enumerate git-tracked files and directories.
Detect imports, includes, package references, CI paths, docs links, Docker, PowerShell, MT4 deployment paths, tests, coverage, and runtime configuration consumers.
Classify generated and runtime-only paths.
acceptance: Every tracked path is represented.
Every path consumer is discoverable or marked needs_review.

tasks.title: Generate initial destination matrix from artifact registry
actions: For each path, propose current owner, evidence refs, target disposition, target path(s), migration wave, rollback path, and validation refs.
Do not assign ambiguous ownership automatically.
acceptance: Exactly one matrix record per tracked path.
All ambiguous records remain explicit.

tasks.title: Resolve multi-module service files
actions: Analyze calendar-ingestor, reentry-engine, shared UI/gateway, transport, and other multi-owner services.
Choose split, single owner, global shared, compatibility layer, or wrapper based on evidence.
Define split specifications before moves.
acceptance: Every multi-owner path has approved disposition and rollback.

tasks.title: Resolve unmapped UI, shared, generated, and global files
actions: Use UI catalog, service source, capability registry, file mapping, and runtime evidence.
Register global ownership where module ownership is inappropriate.
Exclude runtime-generated paths from migration.
acceptance: Unmapped tracked path count = 0.
No runtime-only path is committed as authority.

tasks.title: Freeze final path dispositions
actions: Record HD-SSOT-009.
Approve every move/split/retain/archive operation.
Freeze matrix hash and per-wave scope.
acceptance: Every tracked path has approved final disposition.
needs_review count = 0 for all planned waves.

pass_condition: every consumer has update or compatibility strategy
exit_gate.on_fail: No migration generator may emit executable moves.

phases.name: Generate target tree, rename maps, impact maps, rollback maps, and structural validators
phases.purpose: Replace the hand-authored proposed tree with deterministic registry-derived migration artifacts and prove dry-run safety.

tasks.title: Implement migration renderers
actions: Generate expected module roots, first-level containers, nested profiles, root artifacts, global roots, and target paths from registries.
Use stable sort and LF UTF-8.
Reject collisions and path-length hazards.
acceptance: Same registry inputs produce byte-identical outputs.
No hand-authored target path enters output.

tasks.title: Generate authoritative migration projections
actions: Generate target tree, rename map, rollback map, legacy path index, path impact report, global root inventory, shared-file resolution report, and validation report.
acceptance: All generated artifacts are covered by --check.
Proposed hand-authored tree is replaced as implementation input.

tasks.title: Implement structural and migration validator
actions: Validate 34 roots, unique IDs/locators/symbols, .module-id, manifest identity, containers, profiles, root artifacts, path coverage, global roots, schema authority, rollback entries, stale paths, and generated parity.
Support pre-migration, per-wave, and final modes.
acceptance: All rules in validation_catalog are executable.
Mutation tests fail on each rule violation.

tasks.title: Run report-only dry run without physical moves
actions: Render all operations.
Apply them in a disposable worktree or simulated filesystem.
Run path-consumer rewrites and validation.
Execute rollback.
acceptance: No default working-tree changes.
Forward and rollback simulations pass.
Unexpected path changes = 0.

exit_gate.on_fail: Fix registry facts or generators; never hand-edit generated outputs.

phases.name: Execute controlled pilot migrations
phases.purpose: Prove migration mechanics on representative profiles before repository-wide movement.

tasks.title: Select representative pilot modules
actions: Select at least one single-owner Python module, one documentation/shared-kernel or UI module, and one MT4 or mixed-runtime module if risk permits.
Exclude unresolved shared-file modules.
acceptance: Pilots cover distinct profiles and path-impact types.
Each pilot has complete rollback and tests.

tasks.title: Execute pilot wave in bounded PRs
actions: Create target roots.
Use git mv only for approved operations.
Add .module-id and approved root artifacts.
Update all enumerated consumers.
Regenerate registry projections.
acceptance: No unapproved path changes.
Module, integration, packaging, UI/MT4-specific tests pass.

tasks.title: Execute and verify pilot rollback
actions: Apply rollback map in a disposable branch/worktree.
Run validation against pre-wave baseline.
Confirm history and content hashes preserved.
acceptance: Rollback restores baseline paths and behavior.
No data or history loss.

tasks.title: Approve migration-wave continuation
actions: Review pilot diffs, tests, path impact, and rollback evidence.
Record approval or required generator/policy corrections.
acceptance: Pilot acceptance complete before PHASE-12.

exit_gate.on_fail: Rollback pilots and return to PHASE-08, PHASE-09, or PHASE-10 based on root cause.

phases.name: Execute repository-wide migration waves
phases.purpose: Move the repository incrementally using registry-derived, reviewable, reversible wave maps.

automatic_stop_conditions: MT4 include or terminal deployment failure
calendar deterministic artifact or path violation

tasks.title: Wave 1: governance pointers, generated docs, and compatibility projections
actions: Move or regenerate approved governance and documentation paths.
Update routing, artifact registry, and links without changing domain facts.
acceptance: Authority resolution and docs links pass.

tasks.title: Wave 2: single-owner Python modules
actions: Migrate modules with single-owner files and low shared-runtime risk.
Update imports, packaging, tests, CI, and deployment paths.
acceptance: Python 3.9/3.11 tests pass.
No import or package drift.

tasks.title: Wave 3: UI and shared-kernel modules
actions: Migrate UI backend/frontend and shared-kernel paths according to approved profiles.
Preserve REST/WebSocket, safety, plugin, and shared capability bindings.
acceptance: UI contract and safety-control tests pass.
Shared imports remain valid.

tasks.title: Wave 4: split multi-module services
actions: Apply approved split specifications.
Add compatibility wrappers/adapters where required.
Remove shims only under a separately approved removal condition.
acceptance: All module boundaries and integration tests pass.
No duplicate live implementation remains.

tasks.title: Wave 5: MT4 and mixed-runtime modules
actions: Migrate MQL4 Experts, Indicators, Include, and Python bridge components without renaming runtime-constrained folders.
Update include paths, terminal deployment paths, WebRequest/DDE/file transport configuration, and tests.
acceptance: MQL4 compilation/deployment validation passes.
MT4-Python channel bindings remain correct.
No MQL5 substitutions.

tasks.title: Wave 6: global roots, archive cleanup, and legacy path retirement
actions: Apply global-root policy.
Archive superseded plans and historical artifacts with manifest.
Remove compatibility paths only after zero consumers.
Regenerate final routing and structure docs.
acceptance: Zero active consumer references retired paths.
Archive excluded from normal routing.

pass_condition: every wave passed its tests and structural checks
every wave rollback remained executable until merge
all tracked paths reached approved disposition
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop the current wave, apply its rollback, preserve prior merged waves, and correct registry data or generator logic.

phases.name: Run final clean-clone verification and enable blocking enforcement
phases.purpose: Prove the completed registry and repository are reproducible and make all approved integrity and structure checks blocking.

tasks.title: Run complete clean-clone validation matrix
actions: Run registry build/check, schemas, IDs, references, authority, parity, structure, migration completion, tests, packaging, UI, transport, calendar, MT4, docs links, and git diff checks.
Run on Python 3.9 and 3.11 where applicable.
acceptance: All blocking checks execute and pass.
No skipped or account-blocked job counts as pass.

tasks.title: Prove deterministic regeneration and no-drift state
actions: Run two consecutive full generations.
Verify build manifest hashes.
Tamper each generated-output class in fixtures and prove check mode detects drift without writing.
acceptance: Two builds produce no diff.
All mutation tests pass.

tasks.title: Switch approved validators from report-only to blocking
actions: Make candidate integrity, authority coverage, generated parity, structure, path coverage, and stale-path checks blocking.
Keep only explicitly approved advisory classes non-blocking.
acceptance: Required checks are protected and execute on master/develop or current approved branches.

tasks.title: Publish completion and source-plan supersession record
actions: Record final hashes, PRs, checks, generated artifacts, authority owners, migration results, rollback status, known residuals, and archived source plans.
Update project knowledge summary/router.
acceptance: Source plans no longer appear active.
Final authority and routing are discoverable.

exit_gate.on_fail: The system is not complete; do not close the master plan.

phases.name: Operate the registry system as an ongoing governance process
phases.purpose: Prevent future drift by making registry-first changes, deterministic regeneration, and evidence-backed lifecycle management mandatory.

tasks.title: Adopt registry-first change workflow
actions: Require proposed changes to start with a decision or domain registry update.
Regenerate all projections.
Validate impact before runtime or path changes.
Reject direct edits to generated files.
acceptance: Every accepted change has decision/provenance and generated evidence.

tasks.title: Implement lifecycle operations
actions: Support add, rename, split, merge, deprecate, supersede, retire, and ownership transfer without ID reuse.
Generate crosswalks and compatibility projections.
acceptance: All lifecycle transitions schema-valid and reversible where required.

tasks.title: Implement scheduled drift and routing validation
actions: Regularly check authority paths, missing sources, generated parity, orphan records, stale links, and repository structure.
Notify only on meaningful failures.
acceptance: Drift is detected before release or merge.

tasks.title: Define periodic governance review
actions: Review needs_review items, approved exceptions, external authority freshness, compatibility shims, retired paths, and schema versions.
Create focused issues for unresolved work.
acceptance: No temporary exception becomes permanent without approval.

exit_gate.on_fail: Treat operating governance as incomplete even if the initial migration succeeded.

validation_catalog.description: Exactly one active implementation plan exists.
Every required routed reference resolves or is explicitly blocking; no silent substitution.
Every authored registry and generated artifact parses and validates against its schema.
Record IDs are unique within and across declared namespaces.
Every enabled cross-registry reference resolves.
Two consecutive complete builds are byte-identical and produce no Git diff.
Check mode compares every managed output and writes zero files.
The build manifest excludes its own hash and all listed hashes verify.
All registry paths are repository-relative POSIX paths.
Python 3.9 and 3.11 produce compatible and byte-stable outputs.
Every parity comparison cites a routed authority or explicit unavailable/not_comparable classification.
Blocking cutover mismatches are zero.
Every governed fact group has exactly one active canonical owner.
Every retained external authority is registered, hash-addressable, and non-overlapping.
Exactly 34 active canonical module records exist after ratification.
Module IDs, canonical symbols, and locators are each unique and locators are never derived or reused.
Module-to-process references are valid and no non-process module receives a fabricated step.
Contract identity and linkage live in the contract registry while field shapes have one canonical schema.
Every tracked path has one artifact registry record and approved final disposition.
Every multi-owner or formerly unmapped path has a decision record.
Custom capability records cite the capability registry search and explain why reuse was insufficient.
Each module root matches locator plus kebab-case canonical symbol.
.module-id matches the module registry identity.
Manifest filename, manifest identity, folder identity, and .module-id agree.
Every first-level module container is approved by the module's registry-backed inventory.
Every nested folder is allowed by the selected profile or approved extension.
Every required root artifact exists and prohibited duplicate authority artifacts do not.
Every retained global root complies with the approved global-root policy.
Every authority cutover, move, split, and wrapper has an executable rollback.
Generated current views, graphs, docs, trees, maps, and reports are current and not hand-edited.
No active authority, runtime, CI, packaging, docs, PowerShell, Docker, UI, calendar, or MT4 reference points to a retired path.
No unsupported empty folder is committed.
MQL4 runtime-constrained folders and include/deployment behavior remain valid; no MQL5 substitution occurs.
UI API, WebSocket, safety-control, and implementation bindings remain valid.
Calendar deterministic identities and single-writer rules are not altered by path migration.
MT4-Python ports, protocols, directions, contracts, and owners remain consistent with the registered channel authority.
Archived and superseded files are excluded from normal routing and cannot re-enter authority.
All required checks execute; skipped, neutral, startup-blocked, or account-locked jobs are not passing evidence.
A clean clone regenerates all managed outputs with zero drift.
Future changes follow registry-first lifecycle and regenerate all dependent projections.

definition_of_done: This master JSON is approved and is the only active implementation-sequencing plan.
Source plan duplicates, superseded versions, and reference-only artifacts have recorded dispositions.
The registry engine is deterministic, Python 3.9/3.11 compatible, cross-reference safe, and fully covered by no-write check mode.
Candidate integrity is blocking and passes.
Authority-parity comparison executes deterministically and all cutover-blocking mismatches are resolved.
A schema-valid registry authority manifest assigns exactly one canonical owner to every governed fact group.
All ten authored domain registries are complete, valid, provenance-backed, and cross-linked.
The 34-module universe, canonical symbols, and permanent locators are ratified.
F4, manifest naming, packaging, schema placement, shared-file, and state decisions are closed.
Routing and document authority have completed a governed, reversible cutover.
Every tracked repository path is registered and has an approved final disposition.
The target tree, rename map, impact report, rollback map, legacy-path index, and validation reports are generated deterministically.
Pilot and repository-wide migration waves pass all applicable Python, packaging, UI, calendar, transport, MT4, documentation, and structural checks.
No active path or authority reference points to retired locations.
A clean clone produces zero generated drift.
Blocking CI prevents registry, authority, generated-artifact, and repository-structure drift.
Registry-first lifecycle and periodic governance review are operational.

fail_closed_conditions: A required authority source is missing or ambiguous.
A generated output changes between identical builds.
A record count or stable ID changes without an approved source decision.
A foreign-reference baseline is dirty and enforcement is enabled anyway.
A candidate or parity tool mutates an authority source.
A cutover creates overlapping or missing fact ownership.
A source plan or generated file remains independently editable as authority.
An unmapped or unexpected path appears in a migration wave.
A rollback cannot restore the previous authority or path state.
MT4, UI, calendar, transport, packaging, or runtime behavior fails after migration.
Archive content is routed as current authority.
response: Stop the current phase or wave.
Preserve logs, hashes, diffs, reports, and selected execution profile.
Do not manufacture a green result by deleting references or lowering severity.
Apply the phase-specific rollback when authority or physical paths changed.
Classify the root cause as tool defect, data defect, authority conflict, source drift, path-consumer omission, platform variance, or environmental CI blockage.
Open a focused decision or correction issue under the governing authority.
Resume only from the earliest invalidated gate.

must_report: branch, commit SHA, and PR URL
confirmation that no out-of-scope authority or path changes occurred
must_not_claim: A source plan is current after this master plan is approved.
Candidate registries are canonical before PHASE-07 passes.
Authority parity is proven when routed sources were not loaded.
All conflicts are resolved merely because a report exists.
CI passed when jobs did not start.
Determinism is proven without two consecutive byte-stable builds.
A migration succeeded without rollback evidence and path-consumer validation.
A generated projection is an authored authority.
The project has one SSOT while two sources actively own the same fact.
